title: Dog Unsteady Balance Like Being Drunk: Causes & Emergency Guide
meta_desc: Is your dog wobbling or acting drunk? Learn why sudden loss of balance is a high-priority emergency and how to assist your veterinarian today.
content_html:
What is Ataxia in Dogs?

When a dog appears "drunk," stumbling, or losing their balance, it is medically referred to as ataxia. This condition affects their coordination and can be caused by issues in the inner ear (vestibular system), the brain, or the spinal cord. It often feels frightening to watch, as your dog may lean to one side, tilt their head, or fall over repeatedly while trying to walk.

Is This a Veterinary Emergency?

Yes, sudden loss of balance is considered a high-priority emergency. The urgency level is High. While some causes, such as 'Old Dog Vestibular Disease,' are not life-threatening, other causes like strokes, brain tumors, or the ingestion of toxins (such as marijuana or antifreeze) require immediate medical intervention. If your dog is also vomiting, has flickering eye movements, or seems disoriented, seek emergency care right away.

How Capturing a Video Helps Triage

If it is safe to do so without delaying your trip to the vet, try to capture a quick video of your dog's behavior. A video provides the veterinary team with an objective look at the specific type of stumbling or head tilting your dog is experiencing. Since dogs often act differently due to adrenaline at the clinic, your visual evidence helps the vet differentiate between neurological issues and physical injuries much faster.

faqs.question: Can a dog recover from Vestibular Disease?
faqs.answer: Yes, many dogs with 'Old Dog Vestibular Disease' begin to improve within 48 to 72 hours with supportive care, though a slight head tilt may remain.

faqs.question: What toxins cause a 'drunk' walk in dogs?
faqs.answer: Common toxins include xylitol, marijuana, alcohol, and certain human medications. If you suspect ingestion, bring the packaging to the vet.

faqs.question: Should I try to help my dog walk to the car?
faqs.answer: Use a towel as a sling under their belly to provide support and prevent falls, but be careful as disoriented dogs may accidentally bite if they are scared.
